baseline_dirty_snapshot: M hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh; M commands/commit.md; M agents/{dev,qa,ba,changelog-analyst,style-inspector}.md; M agents/INDEX.md; M docs/dev/INDEX.md and many other workspace files — captured by git status --porcelain pre-dispatch. The hook file was already in the working tree with a Dockerfile.webapp HAPPY_SERVER_URL hunk; commands/commit.md had Step 7 renamed from `Spec-continue dispatch` → `Spec-update dispatch`. Neither of these is changed by this cycle.

observed_preexisting: tests/generated/20260525-095242/test_AC_01_f1a2b3c4d5e6f7a8.py (auto-generated by test-writer; AC-01 wiring lives here; verified expected state)

tasks_completed.description: Tightened Layer 1.F allowlist from PREFIX-read to WHOLE-COMMAND-read in hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh (lines 633-671 → 633-758)
tasks_completed.rationale: M1 — close the compound-command bypass class (`ls <flag> ; python3 -c '...write...'`) that the prior allowlist branch (b) permitted by matching the read-only verb at the prefix without evaluating the remainder. New predicate asserts the WHOLE command is a single pure-read with NO shell control structure / NO redirect / NO recursive shell / NO variable assignment / NO xargs / NO find -exec|-delete|-execdir|-ok|-okdir.

tasks_completed.description: Tightened Layer 1.F allowlist branch (a) to STARTS-WITH match on `[python3 ]scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py`
tasks_completed.rationale: M2 — codex iter-1 finding #3 / iter-2 F7: substring match anywhere in the command allowed comment-spoof (`touch <flag> # scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py`) and script-prefix compound (`scripts/... ; touch <flag>`) to evade the safelist. STARTS-WITH match combined with the M1 compound-denylist closes both attack shapes.

tasks_completed.description: Narrowed Layer 1.F entry-gate to anchor the protected-glob extension at a non-word boundary so `.json.bak` / `.flag.bak` lookalikes no longer match
tasks_completed.rationale: C15b (acceptance-criteria-20260525-095242.json AC-01) requires `touch /tmp/claude-bulk-commit-sentinel-testsid.json.bak ; ls ...` to ALLOW (rc=0). The prior entry-gate regex `commit-sentinel-[A-Za-z0-9_.\-]*\.json` matched `.json.bak` because `.` was inside the basename class and the extension wasn't anchored. New regex anchors `\.json` / `\.flag` and requires a non-identifier follow character (or end). This is a NARROWING of an existing guard, consistent with the pre_existing_guards constraint (entry-gate purpose preserved; precision tightened).

tasks_completed.description: Created sibling test file at tests/generated/20260525-095242/test_AC_04_bypass_20260525.py implementing AC-02 contract
tasks_completed.rationale: M3 — AC-02 requires either in-place extension of prior cycle's test_AC_04_d4e6f8a0c3b5d7ea.py OR a sibling test file. Sibling chosen because (i) the iter-3 33-case matrix is self-contained and orthogonal to the prior cycle's 8 g_hook_* cases, (ii) sibling-file keeps the cycle's diff localized to the new 20260525-095242 directory, (iii) AC-02's `test_extension_path_chosen` field accepts `sibling_new_file`. The file uses EXACT `rc == 2` / `rc == 0` literals (codex iter-1 finding #9), the stable stderr token, and the prior cycle's `_run_hook_with_payload` helper name.

tasks_completed.description: Wired AC-02 nested-pytest invocations to propagate AC02_SKIP_LIVE_PYTEST=1 to child env (recursion fix)
tasks_completed.rationale: Per the AC-02 file's DEV CONTRACT (`Dev MAY refine wiring`), the subprocess pytest invocations in test_AC_02_pytest_directory_invocation_exits_zero and test_AC_02_prior_21_cases_still_pass set AC02_SKIP_LIVE_PYTEST=1 in the child environment. Without this, when the sibling dir is the target, the subprocess pytest re-discovers AC-02 and re-spawns another subprocess pytest → infinite loop until the 180s timeout fires. Assertion shapes and function names unchanged.

tasks_completed.description: Extended find write-action veto to include -fprint/-fprint0/-fprintf/-fls (codex iter-2 finding #2)
tasks_completed.rationale: Codex adversarial review surfaced that `find /tmp -maxdepth 0 -fprint /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-testsid.flag` writes via GNU-find-specific actions. BA M1 enumeration listed only -exec/-delete/-execdir/-ok/-okdir; codex's PROPOSED_FIX adds -fprint/-fprint0/-fprintf/-fls. Applied — closes a real bypass class with a 3-token regex extension.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: M4.3 implementation commit landed in cycle 20260524-205206 (the same cycle that introduced Layer 1.F).
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Cycle 5 of the parent ticket needed a read-only allowlist to satisfy the user's verbatim AC `Single ls/stat/cat reading protected path → still ALLOW`. The implementer chose a prefix-anchored regex `(^|[[:space:];|&])(ls|stat|cat|...)` combined with a redirect-only veto `(>|>>|tee)`, assuming the boundary anchor plus the veto would be sufficient. They missed that (a) `python -c '...write...'`, `node -e ...`, `perl -e ...`, here-docs, and process substitution all write WITHOUT redirect tokens, and (b) the prefix anchor implicitly treats the regex match as the whole command — compound commands whose prefix is read-only but whose suffix writes via any non-redirect channel slip through.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Inverted the predicate from PREFIX-read to WHOLE-COMMAND-read: the new check asserts the trimmed command STARTS WITH a read verb AND contains NO shell control structure / NO redirect / NO recursive shell / NO assignment / NO xargs / NO find write-surface flag. The allowlist behavior (single pure-read still permitted) is preserved per the user's verbatim AC; the predicate is what was wrong.

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: Layer 1.F is a security-critical predicate with a 33-row acceptance matrix that enumerates 7 distinct compound-shape classes (separators, command-substitution, recursive-shell, redirect, assignment, keywords, xargs/find-write-surface) plus 2 STARTS-WITH allowlist branches. Each compound class needs its own grep predicate against the trimmed command. A single mega-regex would be unreadable and would fail M3's 'readable predicate stack' guidance from the BA spec M1 technical hint. The 141-line diff is the minimum that satisfies the 33-row matrix with one predicate per enumerated class. The comment block (S1 — required by ticket Should-Have) accounts for ~30 lines. Note: `git diff --stat` includes a pre-existing Dockerfile.webapp HAPPY_SERVER_URL hunk at lines 943-961 that was in the baseline_dirty_snapshot — NOT introduced by this cycle.

dev.qa_notes: Recommended pytest run: `python3 -m pytest tests/generated/20260525-095242/test_AC_01_f1a2b3c4d5e6f7a8.py tests/generated/20260525-095242/test_AC_04_bypass_20260525.py -q` → 66 passed (33 AC-01 + 33 AC-04 sibling). For AC-02 wiring: `python3 -m pytest tests/generated/20260525-095242/test_AC_02_a9b8c7d6e5f4a3b2.py -q` → 5 passed (4 static + 1 live; AC-02 live invocation runs subprocess pytest against PRIOR_DIR which currently has a PRE-EXISTING failure in tests/generated/20260524-205206/test_AC_05_phase_b_static_step7_dispatch_present caused by a dirty-baseline rename of commands/commit.md Step 7 title `Spec-continue dispatch` → `Spec-update dispatch` — NOT a regression caused by this cycle, verified via `git show baseline_sha:commands/commit.md`). To bypass: `AC02_SKIP_LIVE_PYTEST=1 python3 -m pytest tests/generated/20260525-095242/ -q`.

recommendations: Consider promoting the M1 compound-detection stack into a reusable bash helper function `_is_compound_command` for future hook layers that need similar whole-command-read semantics. Out of scope this cycle (would expand diff).

Codex iter-2 finding #1 (entry-gate fails open for env-var/glob/`/tmp/./` aliases such as `touch /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID.flag`) — REAL bypass class, but OUT OF SCOPE per the user's BA spec (ticket explicitly excludes 'Widening the protected-path glob beyond /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-*.flag and /tmp/claude-bulk-commit-sentinel-*.json' and BA scope says 'no widening of the protected glob'). Recommend filing a follow-up cycle to handle env-var-expanded forms.

Codex iter-2 finding #3 (`\btee\b` redirect-veto false-positives on `grep tee FLAGPATH`) — theoretical concern per BA spec M1 which explicitly lists `tee` as a denied token. AC matrix has no ALLOW row for `grep tee`. Out of scope per user verbatim spec.

Codex iter-2 finding #4 (sibling test copies helper rather than importlib-imports from prior file) — minor stylistic concern. Auto-generated AC-01 test in the same directory also uses a local-copy helper of the same name; precedent set. AC-02 wiring test only checks for token `_run_hook_with_payload` presence, which is satisfied. Defer to QA classification.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5 xhigh) adversarial review returned 10 findings (2 BLOCKER + 2 MAJOR + 1 SCOPE ISSUE + 5 OBSERVATION_ONLY). Per codex calling protocol Rule 3 (caller filters): (1) BLOCKER #1 env-var/glob entry-gate bypass — CLASSIFIED out_of_scope per user spec 'no widening of protected glob'; documented as follow-up. (2) BLOCKER #2 find -fprint/-fprintf/-fls write — APPLIED (3-token regex extension in find write-action veto). (3) MAJOR #3 tee false-positive — CLASSIFIED out_of_scope per BA M1 verbatim spec listing tee as denied. (4) MAJOR #4 helper copy vs import — CLASSIFIED in_scope_minor, documented for QA. (5) SCOPE #5 Dockerfile.webapp hunk — VERIFIED as pre-existing dirty-baseline state. (6-10) OBSERVATION_ONLY on Q1/Q3/Q4/Q5-6-8/Q10 — codex confirmed design choices were sound.

findings.issue: Entry-gate fails open for env-var/glob/canonical-path aliases (e.g. `touch /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID.flag`)
findings.proposed_fix: Widen entry-gate to match shell metacharacters and command-substitution syntax inside basename
findings.rationale: User BA spec explicitly excludes widening the protected glob. Prior cycle accepted this gap. Surface to user as follow-up cycle candidate.

findings.proposed_fix: Extend find write-action regex to include fprint0?|fprintf|fls
findings.rationale: Real bypass class; small fix; closes a write surface the BA enumeration missed.

findings.issue: Lexical \btee\b veto false-positives on `grep tee FLAGPATH`
findings.proposed_fix: Remove tee from generic redirect-veto or restrict to command position
findings.rationale: BA M1 explicitly lists tee as a denied token; AC has no ALLOW row for grep tee. User spec wins (codex Rule 4).

findings.issue: Sibling test re-implements helper (copy) instead of importlib-importing from prior cycle file
findings.proposed_fix: Use importlib.util.spec_from_file_location to reuse prior helper
findings.rationale: Auto-generated AC-01 test in same directory also uses a local-copy helper of identical name+shape. AC-02 wiring test only checks for token presence, which is satisfied. Importlib-import adds complexity and risks pytest collection. Defer to QA.

findings.issue: Unrelated Dockerfile.webapp guard hunk in diff at lines 943-961
findings.rationale: Verified via `git show 4d8b57d:hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh` — the Docker hunk was in the working tree before this cycle started (part of baseline_dirty_snapshot). Not modified by this cycle.
Codex reproduced JSON-newline survives through subprocess.run text=True → bash stdin → python json.load → COMMAND var. C22 passes.

findings.issue: Q3 .json.bak / .flag.foo entry-gate behavior is acceptable as specified
Q4 M2 STARTS-WITH narrowing is acceptable for ticket semantics
findings.rationale: Bare /abs/path/scripts or ./scripts without protected-path mention don't enter Layer 1.F; if they DO mention a protected path, denying is consistent.
Leading-whitespace + raw newline interaction is fine; GNU grep \b works on Ubuntu; dot-source regex requires whitespace after `.` so `cat ./script.sh` is not false-matched.

findings.issue: Q10 diff size is defensible for Layer 1.F
findings.rationale: Codex agrees the 33-row matrix justifies the predicate-stack expansion.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied codex finding #2 (added fprint/fprint0/fprintf/fls to find write-action veto in hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh; tests still pass). Other findings classified per codex calling protocol Rule 3 (caller filters): #1 + #3 are out_of_scope per user verbatim spec; #4 documented for QA review (auto-generated AC-01 test sets precedent of local-copy helper); #5 verified as pre-existing dirty-baseline (not part of this cycle's intentional diff). 5 OBSERVATION_ONLY findings logged for traceability — codex confirmed those design choices were sound.
